lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋਬਨੁ ਧਨੁ ਪ੍ਰਭਤਾ ਕੈ ਮਦ ਮੈ ਅਹਿਨਿਸਿ ਰਹੈ ਦਿਵਾਨਾ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: joban dhan prabhataa kai mad mai ahinis rahai divaanaa |1|
lines.english: In the pride of youth, wealth and glory, day and night, he remains intoxicated. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੀਨ ਦਇਆਲ ਸਦਾ ਦੁਖ ਭੰਜਨ ਤਾ ਸਿਉ ਮਨੁ ਨ ਲਗਾਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: deen deaal sadaa dukh bhanjan taa siau man na lagaanaa |
lines.english: God is merciful to the meek, and forever the Destroyer of pain, but the mortal does not center his mind on Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨ ਨਾਨਕ ਕੋਟਨ ਮੈ ਕਿਨਹੂ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਹੋਇ ਪਛਾਨਾ ॥੨॥੨॥
lines.translit: jan naanak kottan mai kinahoo guramukh hoe pachhaanaa |2|2|
lines.english: O servant Nanak, among millions, only a rare few, as Gurmukh, realize God. ||2||2||
Dhanaasaree, Ninth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਹ ਜੋਗੀ ਕਉ ਜੁਗਤਿ ਨ ਜਾਨਉ ॥
lines.translit: tih jogee kau jugat na jaanau |
lines.english: That Yogi does not know the way.

lines.gurmukhi: ਲੋਭ ਮੋਹ ਮਾਇਆ ਮਮਤਾ ਫੁਨਿ ਜਿਹ ਘਟਿ ਮਾਹਿ ਪਛਾਨਉ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: lobh moh maaeaa mamataa fun jih ghatt maeh pachhaanau |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Understand that his heart is filled with greed, emotional attachment, Maya and egotism. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਰ ਨਿੰਦਾ ਉਸਤਤਿ ਨਹ ਜਾ ਕੈ ਕੰਚਨ ਲੋਹ ਸਮਾਨੋ ॥
lines.translit: par nindaa usatat neh jaa kai kanchan loh samaano |
lines.english: One who does not slander or praise others, who looks upon gold and iron alike,

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਖ ਸੋਗ ਤੇ ਰਹੈ ਅਤੀਤਾ ਜੋਗੀ ਤਾਹਿ ਬਖਾਨੋ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: harakh sog te rahai ateetaa jogee taeh bakhaano |1|
lines.english: who is free from pleasure and pain - he alone is called a true Yogi. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹੁ ਨਾਨਕ ਇਹ ਬਿਧਿ ਕੋ ਜੋ ਨਰੁ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਤਾਹਿ ਤੁਮ ਮਾਨੋ ॥੨॥੩॥
lines.translit: kahu naanak ih bidh ko jo nar mukat taeh tum maano |2|3|
lines.english: Says Nanak, whoever knows this technique is judged to be liberated. ||2||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਚੰਚਲ ਮਨੁ ਦਹ ਦਿਸਿ ਕਉ ਧਾਵਤ ਅਚਲ ਜਾਹਿ ਠਹਰਾਨੋ ॥
lines.translit: chanchal man deh dis kau dhaavat achal jaeh tthaharaano |
lines.english: The restless mind wanders in the ten directions - it needs to be pacified and restrained.
Dhanaasaree, Ninth Mehl:
Now, what efforts should I make?

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਹ ਬਿਧਿ ਮਨ ਕੋ ਸੰਸਾ ਚੂਕੈ ਭਉ ਨਿਧਿ ਪਾਰਿ ਪਰਉ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: jih bidh man ko sansaa chookai bhau nidh paar prau |1| rahaau |
lines.english: How can I dispel the anxieties of my mind? How can I cross over the terrifying world-ocean? ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨਮੁ ਪਾਇ ਕਛੁ ਭਲੋ ਨ ਕੀਨੋ ਤਾ ਤੇ ਅਧਿਕ ਡਰਉ ॥
lines.translit: janam paae kachh bhalo na keeno taa te adhik ddrau |
lines.english: Obtaining this human incarnation, I have done no good deeds; this makes me very afraid!

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸੁਨਿ ਕਛੁ ਗਿਆਨੁ ਨ ਉਪਜਿਓ ਪਸੁ ਜਿਉ ਉਦਰੁ ਭਰਉ ॥
lines.translit: guramat sun kachh giaan na upajio pas jiau udar bhrau |
lines.english: I listened to the Guru's Teachings, but spiritual wisdom did not well up within me; like a beast, I fill my belly.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨ ਬਚ ਕ੍ਰਮ ਹਰਿ ਗੁਨ ਨਹੀ ਗਾਏ ਯਹ ਜੀਅ ਸੋਚ ਧਰਉ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: man bach kram har gun nahee gaae yeh jeea soch dhrau |1|
lines.english: In thought, word and deed, I have not sung the Lord's Praises; this thought worries my mind. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹੁ ਨਾਨਕ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਬਿਰਦੁ ਪਛਾਨਉ ਤਬ ਹਉ ਪਤਿਤ ਤਰਉ ॥੨॥੪॥੯॥੯॥੧੩॥੫੮॥੪॥੯੩॥
lines.translit: kahu naanak prabh birad pachhaanau tab hau patit trau |2|4|9|9|13|58|4|93|
lines.english: Says Nanak, O God, please confirm Your Law of Grace; for only then can I, the sinner, be saved. ||2||4||9||9||13||58||4||93||
One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:
Dhanaasaree, First Mehl, Second House, Ashtpadheeyaa:
Within this pool, the swans find their Lord, the Lord of their souls. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਰਸੁ ਚੋਗ ਚੁਗਹਿ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਭਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: har ras chog chugeh prabh bhaavai |
lines.english: They taste the subtle essence of the Lord; they are loved by God.
The Saints gather in the Ambrosial Nectar; they do not go far away from there.
The Guru is the ocean, filled with pearls.
What can the poor crane accomplish by bathing in the mud puddle?

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੀਚੜਿ ਡੂਬੈ ਮੈਲੁ ਨ ਜਾਇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: keecharr ddoobai mail na jaae |1| rahaau |
lines.english: It sinks into the mire, and its filth is not washed away. ||1||Pause||
After careful deliberation, the thoughtful person takes a step.
He obtains the treasure of liberation, and enjoys the sublime essence of the Lord.
Forsaking duality, he becomes a devotee of the Formless Lord.
His comings and goings end, and the Guru protects him. ||2||
In loving devotional worship, they merge in the Celestial Lord.
The swan do not leave this pool.
They speak the Unspoken Speech, and they honor and revere the Guru's Word. ||3||
The Yogi, the Primal Lord, sits within the celestial sphere of deepest Samaadhi.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਰਵਰ ਮਹਿ ਹੰਸੁ ਹੰਸ ਮਹਿ ਸਾਗਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: saravar meh hans hans meh saagar |
lines.english: The swans are in the pool, and the pool is in the swans.
The three worlds continue to center their attention on His Light.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਰਿ ਨ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਕਹਹੁ ਕੋਊ ਕੈਸੇ ॥
lines.translit: naar na purakh kahahu koaoo kaise |
lines.english: He is not male, and He is not female; how can anyone describe Him?
The silent sages and the Yogic masters seek the Sanctuary of the True Lord. ||4||
Subduing ego, one meets the Lord, and places his feet on the Path. ||5||
The Gurmukhs worship and contemplate the Celestial Lord.
The Lord is the source of bliss, the support of the helpless.
God is the Lover of His devotees, the Destroyer of fear.
Destined only to die, he comes into the world.
He makes many efforts, but still, the Messenger of Death tortures him.
